title: Kimi Code is good at video reasoning with Kimi K2.6 🎬
summary: Kimi Code, powered by Kimi K2.6, enables video reasoning tasks by accepting reference videos and generating color grading LUT files. Users can query visual properties like colors, shots, and style from uploaded videos to produce production-ready outputs.
writeup: This short clip demonstrates Kimi's video analysis capability, focused on color grading workflows. A user drags reference video clips into Kimi and prompts it to analyze them against the Rec. 709 standard, and Kimi outputs a .cube LUT file ready for direct import into DaVinci Resolve, CapCut, or Adobe Premiere. The practical angle is AI-assisted color matching from reference footage rather than manual grading from scratch.
key_points: Kimi can accept dragged video files and answer questions about color, shot composition, or scene structure directly from the footage.
Prompting Kimi to analyze reference clips against the Rec. 709 standard produces a .cube LUT file as output, a standard color grading format.
The generated .cube file is compatible with DaVinci Resolve, CapCut, and Adobe Premiere, making the workflow immediately actionable in common editing tools.
Long-form video (such as a podcast) can be condensed to a set of highlights, suggesting Kimi handles both analytical and summarization tasks over video input.
